data.title: OSL Group Names New Company Secretary and Authorised Representative
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

OSL Group Limited ( (HK:0863) ) has provided an announcement.

OSL Group Limited has announced a change in its corporate governance team, appointing Mr. Ko Sebastian Yat Fung as company secretary and authorised representative in Hong Kong effective 24 July 2026. Mr. Ko, who joined the group in April 2026 and leads its legal team for international businesses, brings over 15 years of legal experience and multiple professional qualifications, while the board expressed its appreciation for outgoing company secretary Ms. Kuo Yuen Fan’s contributions and signalled continuity in regulatory and compliance oversight.

The appointment underscores OSL’s focus on strengthening its legal and compliance capabilities as it manages international operations and ongoing obligations under Hong Kong listing rules. Investors and other stakeholders can interpret the move as an effort to reinforce governance infrastructure and ensure robust handling of regulatory processes, potentially supporting the group’s strategic execution and risk management as its business evolves.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0863) stock is a Buy
with a HK$18.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on OSL Group Limited stock,
see the HK:0863 Stock Forecast page.

More about OSL Group Limited

OSL Group Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 863, operates as a diversified group with international business interests. The company is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a conventional Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure.

Average Trading Volume: 1,643,085

data.title: Qinqin Foodstuffs Issues Profit Warning as Revenue and Margins Slide
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company ( (HK:1583) ) is now available.

Qinqin Foodstuffs Group has warned that its unaudited revenue for the six months ended 30 June 2026 is expected to fall about 8% year-on-year to roughly RMB485 million, with profit attributable to equity shareholders plunging around 73% to 82% to between RMB4 million and RMB6 million. The company cited weaker sales through traditional retail channels amid softer consumer spending, lower sales volumes of jelly and rice wine products that reduced factory utilisation and economies of scale, and a related RMB22 million drop in gross profit and a two-percentage-point decline in gross margin, partly offset by stronger sales to snack food chains and growth in its OEM business, prompting a cautionary note to shareholders and investors as final results are still being finalised.

The company noted that the interim figures are based on preliminary, unaudited management accounts and may be adjusted before the formal results announcement scheduled for mid-August 2026. Management urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading the company’s shares pending publication of the finalised interim results, highlighting the significant profitability pressure despite some resilience from newer channels and OEM operations.

More about Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company

Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed food manufacturer focused on snack foods, jelly products and rice wine, serving both traditional retail channels and snack food chains. The group also operates an OEM manufacturing business, positioning it as a contract producer for other brands within the broader packaged food sector.

Average Trading Volume: 53,284

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$702.2M

A & S Group (Holdings) Limited has called its annual general meeting for 15 September 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will consider adopting the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 March 2026 and review the reports of the board and external auditor. The meeting will also vote on reappointing HLB Hodgson Impey Cheng Limited as auditor, re-electing one executive and one independent non-executive director, authorising the board to set directors’ pay, and granting a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, which could expand fundraising flexibility while diluting existing holdings within defined limits.

The agenda reflects a standard corporate governance cycle focused on continuity of board leadership and external oversight through auditor reappointment. The proposed share issuance mandate would give directors additional scope to respond to capital needs or market opportunities during the mandate period, with constraints aligned to Hong Kong listing regulations and existing shareholder protections.

data.title: China Life revamps profit distribution in connected investment partnership
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Life Insurance Co ( (HK:2628) ) has shared an announcement.

China Life Insurance Company Limited has announced a change to the profit distribution mechanism of a connected investment partnership formed with China Life Jiangsu, Jiangsu Fund and China Life Property and Casualty Insurance. The partners plan to sign a supplemental agreement by the end of 2026, shifting from project-based profit allocation to a model based on the overall performance of the partnership, aligning more closely with market practices.

Under the new structure, capital will first be returned to limited partners, then to the general partner, followed by an 8% annualized return hurdle for both before remaining excess income is split 80% to limited partners and 20% to the general partner. The board considers the revised terms fair, commercially reasonable and beneficial to shareholders, noting that several directors with roles in related entities abstained from voting on the resolution to avoid conflicts of interest.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2628) stock is a Buy
with a HK$35.00 price target.

see the HK:2628 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Life Insurance Co

China Life Insurance Company Limited is a leading life insurance provider in China, operating as a joint stock company with limited liability and listed in Hong Kong. The company focuses on life insurance and related financial services, supported by its state-owned controlling shareholder China Life Insurance (Group) Company and its affiliated subsidiaries.

data.title: China Life renews connected insurance sales pact with CLP&C
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Life Insurance Co ( (HK:2628) ) has shared an update.

China Life Insurance Company Limited plans to renew and extend its insurance sales cooperation with affiliate China Life Property & Casualty through a new Insurance Sales Framework Agreement. Under the arrangement, China Life will continue to act as an agent for CLP&C, selling a wide spectrum of property and casualty products, including compulsory, automobile and various commercial and agricultural policies, and collecting premiums in CLP&C’s name.

Because CLP&C is an associate of the controlling shareholder, the renewed arrangement is classified as a continuing connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, triggering reporting, announcement and annual review obligations but not independent shareholder approval. However, as cumulative dealings with the parent group exceed 5% of China Life’s latest audited net assets, the new framework requires shareholder approval under Shanghai Stock Exchange rules and, once approved, will replace the existing agreement from 1 January 2027, providing continuity to the group’s distribution and agency income streams.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2628) stock is a Buy
with a HK$35.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Life Insurance Co stock,
see the HK:2628 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Life Insurance Co

China Life Insurance Company Limited is a leading Chinese life insurer listed in Hong Kong, providing a broad range of insurance products and related services. The company’s controlling shareholder, China Life Insurance (Group) Company, holds about 68.37% of its issued share capital, and the group maintains close operational ties with its affiliates, including China Life Property & Casualty (CLP&C).

Average Trading Volume: 56,301,693

data.title: SG Group Holdings Faces Mandatory Cash Offer in Hong Kong Takeover
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from SG Group Holdings Ltd ( (HK:1657) ).

SG Group Holdings Limited, a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1657, operates in the professional services and staffing sector, providing human resources solutions to corporate clients in the region. It is now the target of a change-of-control transaction led by Hong Kong Weiye Software Co., Limited, an entity incorporated in Hong Kong that is pursuing ownership of the company through a regulated takeover process.

The company and Hong Kong Weiye Software have jointly announced the despatch of a composite document detailing an unconditional mandatory cash offer for all SG Group Holdings shares not already held by the offeror and its concert parties. The offer opened on 24 July 2026 and is scheduled to close on 14 August 2026, with the documentation including recommendations from an independent board committee and advice from an independent financial adviser, signalling a formal and closely supervised takeover that could reshape SG Group’s governance, board composition and leadership roles.

More about SG Group Holdings Ltd

SG Group Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1657, operates in the professional services and staffing industry. The company focuses on providing human resources solutions and related services to corporate clients, positioning itself within Hong Kong’s broader services sector and subject to local securities and takeover regulations.

Hong Kong Weiye Software Co., Limited, incorporated in Hong Kong, serves as the offeror in the current transaction. Through a mandatory cash offer for all issued shares of SG Group Holdings not already owned or agreed to be acquired by the offeror and its concert parties, Hong Kong Weiye Software is seeking effective control of the listed company within the framework of Hong Kong’s Takeovers Code.

The latest update is out from United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ).

United Company RUSAL has entered into new supplementary electrical grid connection contracts with associates of En+ for its Bratsk Aluminium Smelter and an engineering and construction subsidiary. The agreements formalize the purchase of technological connection services to support power-receiving equipment and development facilities, reflecting the group’s ongoing need for reliable grid access to sustain its core operations.

The contracts mainly address adjustments following Russia’s increase in VAT from 20% to 22%, with the supplementary agreements covering only the incremental VAT on previously paid consideration. Although the estimated consideration for 2026 and 2027 excluding VAT is zero, the updated terms underscore compliance with regulated tariffs and cost-based pricing, with modest additional tax payments scheduled, indicating limited direct financial impact but continued alignment with regulatory changes and connected-party transaction rules.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is a major global aluminium producer, operating smelters and related industrial facilities with a focus on energy-intensive metal production. MOG Holdings Limited ( (HK:1942) ) just unveiled an update.

MOG Digitech Holdings has warned that it expects to post an unaudited consolidated net loss of at least RMB30 million for the five months to 31 May 2026, implying a loss for the first half of 2026 versus a net profit of about RMB1.9 million a year earlier. Management attributed the downturn to a sharp fall in revenue and gross profit, driven by intense competition in China’s digital payment sector, reduced funding capacity in its financing services, and lower purchase rebates in its optical retail and licensing operations.

TVB Limited has officially changed its English name from Television Broadcasts Limited to TVB Limited and its Chinese name from 電視廣播有限公司 to 無綫集團有限公司, effective 24 July 2026, following earlier shareholder approvals. Existing share certificates bearing the former name will remain valid for legal title, trading, settlement, registration and delivery, with new certificates issued only under the new name going forward.

The company emphasized that the name change does not affect the rights of shareholders or security holders, and it will later announce details of a new Chinese stock short name once confirmed by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. TVB also updated its registered address to reflect the renaming of “TVB City” to “TVB,” while keeping the physical location of its registered office unchanged, signalling a brand consolidation without operational disruption.

More about Television Broadcasts Limited

TVB Limited, formerly known as Television Broadcasts Limited, is a Hong Kong-based media company primarily engaged in television broadcasting and related entertainment services. The company focuses on serving audiences in Hong Kong and broader Chinese-speaking markets, operating from its facilities in Tseung Kwan O Industrial Estate in the New Territories.

HBM Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2142) ) just unveiled an update.

HBM Holdings issued a positive profit alert, indicating that unaudited revenue for the six months ended 30 June 2026 is expected to rise to between US$120 million and US$125 million from US$101 million a year earlier, with profit projected at US$62 million to US$67 million and adjusted profit between US$70 million and US$75 million. Management attributed the improved performance to expanded strategic collaborations with major pharma partners, new out-licensing and milestone payments on innovative products, and robust growth at Nona Biosciences, underscoring the company’s strengthening position in antibody discovery and its dependence on partnership-driven revenues, while cautioning that final audited results may differ.
